name: Disclosure Project 2001
description: The Disclosure Project, spearheaded by Dr. Steven Greer, culminated in a press conference at the National Press Club in Washington D.C. on May 9, 2001. The project aimed to publicly disclose alleged evidence of UFOs, extraterrestrial life, and suppressed advanced technologies. Greer presented testimony from over twenty former government and military officials, claiming they had direct knowledge of UFO sightings, crash retrievals, reverse engineering programs, and a decades-long cover-up by governments worldwide. Proponents argue that these technologies, if released, could solve global energy and environmental problems. Mainstream perspectives largely dismiss the Disclosure Project's claims as unsubstantiated, citing a lack of verifiable evidence and questioning the credibility of the witnesses. Skeptics point out inconsistencies in testimonies, lack of corroborating physical evidence, and the potential for misinterpretation of events by witnesses. The project has been criticized for relying on anecdotal evidence and lacking rigorous scientific methodology. While Greer's efforts have garnered significant attention within the UFO community, they have failed to gain widespread acceptance or validation from the scientific or governmental establishment. The event continues to be a focal point for believers in government UFO cover-ups, while being viewed with skepticism by the broader scientific community.
subtitle: Unveiling alleged UFO secrecy in 2001

claims.claim: Governments are actively covering up the existence of UFOs and extraterrestrial life.
claims.evidence: Testimony from former government and military officials describing UFO sightings and crash retrievals.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete physical evidence, inconsistencies in witness accounts, potential for misidentification of conventional aircraft or natural phenomena.

claims.claim: Advanced technologies derived from extraterrestrial sources are being suppressed to maintain the current energy paradigm.
claims.evidence: Claims of reverse-engineered UFO technology capable of providing clean and limitless energy.
claims.criticism: Absence of verifiable technological documentation or prototypes, reliance on anecdotal evidence and speculation.

claims.claim: The cover-up is motivated by economic and political interests, particularly the control of energy resources.
claims.evidence: Allegations of secret government agencies and corporations working to suppress free energy technologies.
claims.criticism: Lack of credible evidence linking government agencies to the suppression of free energy, reliance on conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Steven Greer
keyFigures.role: Founder and Director of the Disclosure Project

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Nuclear physicist and UFO researcher, critical of some aspects of Greer's approach

mythologyConnections: Myths of celestial beings visiting Earth

timeline.event: Dr. Steven Greer begins gathering testimonies from alleged government and military witnesses.
The Disclosure Project press conference takes place at the National Press Club in Washington D.C.
Greer continues to promote the Disclosure Project through books, documentaries, and speaking engagements.

sources.author: Steven M. Greer
sources.work: Disclosure: Military and Government Witnesses Reveal the Greatest Secrets in Modern History
sources.title: Steven M. Greer
